item.title: Turkey unveils new ICBM — touted as able to hit the US mainland
item.description: Promotional AI video showed Yıldırımhan missile seemingly striking nuclear sites in America well beyond its range

item.title: Jane Street earned $10bn in first quarter as it doubled trading revenue
item.description: Results cement proprietary firm as one of the most profitable on Wall Street

item.title: LNG carriers test Iran blockade in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Pakistan cancelled plans to buy cargoes on spot market because it expected Gulf shipments to resume

item.title: Billionaire James Packer among backers of Architect’s OnlyFans deal
item.description: Australian mogul’s funds to invest in Architect Capital SPV taking a minority stake in platform

item.title: While AI pumps up portfolios, some companies sound the alarm
item.description: The clout of Big Tech groups is obscuring the precarious state of others

item.title: Pentagon publishes dozens of files on alleged UFOs
item.description: The public can ‘make up their own minds’ on strange sightings, says US defence department

item.title: Porsche cuts 500 jobs and closes its electric bike motor division
item.description: Luxury car maker retrenching to focus on core sports vehicle business

item.title: David Einhorn wants ladies in ‘sexy party clothes’ at his hedge fund party
item.description: Sorry, what year is it?

item.title: Ryan Cohen, GameStop’s rebel CEO
item.description: His $56bn hostile bid for eBay has baffled Wall Street, but it could be the world’s first meme stock-driven deal

item.title: Activist Oasis builds its Capita stake to 15% before AGM
item.description: Hong Kong-based investor had steadily increased its exposure to UK outsourcer via swaps

item.title: Chris Hohn’s hedge fund slashes $8bn Microsoft stake in warning over AI disruption
item.description: TCI has cut its position in tech giant from 10% to 1%

item.title: UBS boss warns of European decline with ‘over-regulation across the board’
item.description: Sergio Ermotti tells FT it would take a ‘very profound and painful crisis’ to pressure politicians to take action

item.title: Uber-backed electric bike start-up Lime files for IPO
item.description: Operator of bright green e-bikes and scooters plans US listing this year

item.title: The fusion power race is heating up
item.description: Start-ups ride a funding wave as governments eye transformative potential

item.title: Will AI help the Fed conquer inflation? With Austan Goolsbee
item.description: The Chicago Fed head talks GPTs, the rate outlook, and new Fed nominee Kevin Warsh

item.title: The energy shock is not all about price
item.description: Low stocks of crude and products in Europe are bigger risks for the continent

item.title: Stellantis expands Chinese alliance in bid to secure Spanish plants
item.description: Carmaker deepens collaboration with Leapmotor over two facilities employing nearly 6,000 people

item.title: South East Water chief quits after calls for management clear-out
item.description: Utility has been heavily criticised over supply failures and outages

item.title: Commerzbank raises targets to boost defences against UniCredit takeover
item.description: Italian bank has built a roughly 30% stake in Germany’s second-largest listed lender

item.title: British Airways owner IAG warns Iran war will add €2bn to jet fuel bill
item.description: Company plans to recoup about 60% of higher costs through savings and raising ticket prices

item.title: Toyota warns of $4.2bn hit from Middle East war
item.description: World’s biggest carmaker sells record 10.5mn vehicles last year on strong demand for hybrids

item.title: Sony says cost of developing PlayStation successor will hit gaming profits
item.description: Fourth-quarter profit misses expectations amid impairments as $3.2bn stock buyback announced

item.title: Quarterly reporting: when is less important than what
item.description: Plus, Norway’s rate increase. And dishwashers.

item.title: British-Chinese nationals found guilty of spying for Hong Kong
item.description: Two men, including former Border Force official, convicted in trial at London’s Old Bailey

item.title: FirstFT: Reform UK surges in early election results
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: US-Iran truce strained and ‘potential’ EU talks with Putin

item.title: Big money, hot markets and ‘blissful ignorance’ at Milken
item.description: Financiers convening in Beverly Hills were brimming with optimism in spite of the Iran war and stress in private markets

item.title: Trump’s World Cup chief defends high ticket prices
item.description: ‘We don’t really believe in price controls’ says Andrew Giuliani, despite outcry from fan groups

item.title: US and South Africa hold talks on mining deals after year of tensions
item.description: Officials discussed resources investments as Washington tries to break China’s control of critical minerals production

item.title: US businesses urge Trump to intervene over new EU consumer rules
item.description: American companies fear an update to Brussels’ ‘Product Liability Directive’ will make it easier for consumers to sue

item.title: The jury is still out on AI in finance
item.description: Recruiting digital natives with critical thinking skills is going to be crucial
